key_insight: MaterEval turns expert materials rules into paired preference signals so LLMs can learn to make evidence-backed evaluations instead of blind guesses.
engineering_relevance: This creates a low-cost, deployable evaluation layer for autonomous materials discovery workflows, especially when screening huge candidate sets like high-entropy alloys.
method: Generate two judgments for each candidate: an informed, rule-based evaluation with evidence and a blind, rule-removed guess.
Use the paired outputs as preference data to train general-purpose LLMs toward more reliable materials assessment.
Add a fast-slow reasoning scheme to separate large-scale screening from deeper review on a smaller subset.
evidence_signal: In the high-entropy alloy case study, small open-source LLMs improved accuracy, conclusion consistency, and evidence discrimination without external retrieval, approaching rule-based closed-source models.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of encoded expert rules, and the approach may transfer less well to materials domains with sparse or ambiguous criteria.
paper_title: From Blind Guess to Informed Judgment: Teaching LLMs to Evaluate Materials by Building Knowledge-Augmented Preference Signals
